meta_title: For Students: user onboarding to Solve no testing knowledge (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome no testing knowledge. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Master User Onboarding: Overcome No Testing Knowledge (Tutorial)
content.seo_description: Bridge the testing gap by mastering user onboarding. A student-focused tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code to build professional, reliable SaaS flows.
content.answer_block: You don't need a PhD in QA to ship reliable software. By implementing automated user onboarding flows, you create a controlled environment that validates user actions. Use Antigravity to scaffold your onboarding logic and Claude Code to generate unit tests that ensure your app behaves exactly as expected.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks coding your first SaaS, but every deployment feels like a leap of faith. The fear of breaking production isn't just a technical hurdle—it's a psychological barrier that halts your growth as an engineer. When you lack formal testing knowledge, the silence after a commit is deafening.

But here is the secret: you don't need to be a QA expert to write robust code. By focusing on user onboarding, you build a sandbox that forces you to define clear user paths. This isn't just about design; it's about engineering reliability into the very first interaction a user has with your product.

steps.title: Define the Onboarding State Machine
steps.description: Map out the user journey. By defining the states, you create a blueprint that makes testing logical rather than intimidating.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a state machine definition for a SaaS onboarding flow (Welcome, ProfileSetup, FeatureTour, Dashboard). Include transitions and potential error states.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate a TypeScript state machine for the onboarding flow defined in Antigravity.'

steps.title: Instrument the User Path
steps.description: Add hooks to your onboarding components so you can track progress. This allows you to 'test' the user's journey in real-time.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate React hooks to track onboarding progress and emit events when a user completes a step in the defined state machine.

steps.title: Generate Automated Smoke Tests
steps.description: Use Claude Code to read your onboarding logic and write 'smoke tests' that verify the happy path exists.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a test plan that covers the primary onboarding path, including edge cases like network failures or missing profile data.

steps.title: Simulate User Interactions
steps.description: Go beyond simple unit tests by simulating actual user clicks and form submissions within your test suite.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Playwright script that navigates through the onboarding flow and verifies the final state is 'Dashboard'.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Create a Playwright test file for the onboarding flow based on the current implementation.'

steps.title: Integrate Verification into CI
steps.description: Automate the testing process so that every time you save, your onboarding flow is verified.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a GitHub Actions YAML file that runs the onboarding smoke tests on every push to the main branch.

pro_tips: Test the 'empty state' first; if your app works with no data, it's 80% more resilient.
Use mock servers for external APIs during onboarding to keep tests fast and deterministic.
Focus on 'behavioral testing' over 'implementation testing' to avoid brittle code that breaks during refactors.
Record your tests as screen captures to visually debug onboarding drops.
Treat your test code with the same care as your production code—it is the life insurance for your SaaS.
resources: Testing JavaScript by Kent C. Dodds: https://testingjavascript.com
content.next_steps: Once your onboarding flow is tested, move on to implementing automated error logging to catch issues before your users do.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer a beginner guessing if your code works; you are an engineer building with intent. Take your portfolio to the next level by shipping code that lasts. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
